# Weekly Review — 2026-W25 (June 16–22)

## What happened

Three study sessions this week, all on M07 (Computability Theory). Made real progress —
moved from competency 0 (aware of the field but no formal grounding) to competency 2
(can apply the halting problem proof and explain the TM model). Par for M07 is 45 hours;
logged 6.5 hours this week.

Also posted the inaugural X thread (7 tweets, PhD curriculum announcement) and ran the
Reddit r/learnmachinelearning post.

---

## Sessions

| Date | Module(s) | Hours | Energy | Competency change | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Jun 17 | M07 | 2.5 | medium | 1 → 2 | Halting problem clicked |
| Jun 19 | M07 | 2.0 | high | — | Rice's theorem, Church-Turing |
| Jun 21 | M07 | 2.0 | medium | — | Review + retrieval practice |

---

## Key insights this week

1. The halting problem's diagonalization argument is more intuitive than it first appears — once
   you see that "encoding a TM as a string" is not a trick but a genuine mathematical move.
2. Church-Turing thesis is not a theorem — it's a claim about what "computation" means, and it
   shapes how we think about LLM expressiveness.
3. High-energy sessions genuinely produce more lasting comprehension than medium-energy sessions
   of equal length. First time noticing this pattern in data.

---

## What didn't happen (and why it's fine)

- Phase B workflow not yet launched (was planning to this week). Blocked on implementing
  `phd-phase-b.js` — this takes a high-energy work block, and this week's sessions went
  into studying instead. Good trade.
- M01 Linear Algebra not started. M07 is more engaging right now and that's fine — the
  curriculum explicitly supports non-sequential study.

---

## Energy pattern observation

Three medium/high sessions. No low-energy days this week. This is atypical — the new
curriculum launch probably added extra motivation. Watch for regression to mean next week.

RQ5 note: the high-energy session (Jun 19) covered significantly more ground than the
medium-energy sessions. The ratio feels like roughly 1.5x output per hour.

---

## Next week's one priority

Complete M07: cover Rice's theorem depth, do /oral-exam M07, and write the blog post seed.
M07 completion is the gate for Post 4 and for the first proper RQ5 data point.

---

## Suggested Monday MVP

Open M07/theory.md to the Rice's theorem section and read for 20 minutes.
That's it. That's enough for a Monday.
